description_long: This sculpture features two classical human figures, both partially damaged and missing significant portions of their bodies. The figure on the left appears to be a male, standing upright with a muscular build, missing parts of the head, arms, and torso, and is mounted on a rectangular plinth. The figure on the right resembles a draped female form, reminiscent of Greco-Roman statuary, with missing head and arm sections, and is depicted in a contrapposto stance with flowing garments. The surface of both figures is dark, likely bronze or a similar metal, with areas of white where material is missing or eroded. The overall appearance is aged and weathered, suggesting exposure to the elements or intentional artistic distress. The base is solid and geometric, providing stability for the upright male figure.
description: A pair of damaged classical-style human sculptures, one male and one draped female, both with missing sections and a dark, weathered surface. The male stands on a rectangular plinth, while the female is depicted in flowing garments.
description_view_0: Front view showing both figures, with the male on the left standing upright and the draped female on the right, both visibly damaged and missing parts.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the male figure and the back of the female figure, with clear visibility of missing head and arm sections.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of both figures, emphasizing the draped garment details and the extent of missing material.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the profile of the female figure and the back of the male, with further evidence of surface damage and missing parts.
attribute_subject_matter: classical human figures, one male and one draped female
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the background is neutral and does not indicate an outdoor or indoor context.
